brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid reasoning comparing 30% vs 75%; Goes one level beyond surface by laying out causal structure and computing the difference; No self-monitoring or reflection on reasoning process; Mentions the comparison as implicit verification but doesn't check from alternative angles; Presents conclusion confidently without acknowledging assumptions about the counterfactual framework; Acknowledges both actions but doesn't explore alternative interpretations of the counterfactual question; Full derivation trail with causal diagram, probability table, and explicit conclusion
